title: TELOS authorization chain — every authz run, its record, and its outcome
note: One entry per authorization run on disk. Refusals are PRESERVED, never erased — five of eight runs refused authorization, which is the fail-closed posture demonstrated, not a failure history to clean up. Entries with a plan_path are re-hashed from disk by the verifier; authz-001..003 reviewed candidate bytes that are not preserved as matured-plan files on main, so their plan_ref is record-internal only (checked against the record, not the disk) — recorded honestly rather than reconstructed.

chain.block_cause: codex decision 'revise' (required-seat dissent); AM-41's blanket hashbang exclusion contradicted Task 5's weave.mjs shebang

why: A future model must be able to walk authorization history without inferring outcomes from filenames or assuming success. The chain shows the gate refusing five times — including refusing a Daedalus-converged plan on one required seat's dissent (authz-007) — and shows every run under trust_mode signed. That record IS the trust model, demonstrated.
change_rule: A new authorization appends an entry (council run -> record committed under docs/runs/ -> CURRENT-AUTHORITY.json updated -> this contract updated in the same change or verify-contracts fails). Entries are never rewritten.

oracle.source_of_truth: the authorization-summary.json records on disk + CURRENT-AUTHORITY.json
oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (each record exists, parses, and matches this entry; plan_path entries re-hashed from disk; exactly one active entry equal to CURRENT-AUTHORITY.active_authorization; superseded statuses cross-checked)
